title: huperzia parksii
intro: Huperzia parksii, also known as the New Zealand liverwort or Queensland liverwort, is a type of non-vascular plant that belongs to the Huperziaceae family. It has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, particularly for its potential to improve cognitive function and memory. The plant contains a bioactive compound called huperzine A, which has been found to have neuroprotective and antioxidant properties, contributing to its health benefits, such as improved brain function, reduced inflammation, and enhanced nerve regeneration. Huperzia parksii can be prepared as an herbal supplement in capsule or tablet form, and also as an extract that can be added to food or drinks.
